title: GoThru Photographer API Go Project Report

issues.display_title: Issue #38: Fix: Create Portfolio 500 "invalid character 'ï'" on Gothru/ Tourmkr Thumbnail Fetch (BOM)
issues.main_description_html:
RESULT 02/07/26

✅ Implementation: Portfolio Thumbnail Fetch BOM Fix

Overview

This document outlines the root cause and the implemented solution for the issue where creating a portfolio item (Gothru/Tourmkr) resulted in a 500 Internal Server Error due to a UTF-8 Byte Order Mark (BOM) in the upstream API response.

Task Reference

Project Board: Photographer API GO (Project 104)
Task Title: Fix: Create Portfolio 500 "invalid character 'A_'" on Gothru/ Tourmkr Thumbnail Fetch (BOM)

1. The Problem

When users attempted to create a portfolio item using a tourmkr.com value (type: 3), the system triggered a fetch for a thumbnail via resolveThumbnailForType. This internally calls the upstream API (https://apil.gothru.co/virtualtours/public/first-pano).

The upstream API occasionally returned JSON prefixed with a UTF-8 Byte Order Mark (BOM: EF BB BF). Because encoding/json in Go's standard library does not handle BOMs implicitly when parsing JSON, the json.NewDecoder().Decode() execution failed with the error:
invalid character 'A_' looking for beginning of value

This error propagated up to the main handler, failing the entire portfolio creation process with a 500 Internal Server Error, even though the thumbnail is an optional field.

2. Implementation Details

The fix was implemented in two distinct layers to ensure resilience:

Part 1: Stripping the BOM Before JSON Parsing

In src/action/v1/portfolio/portfolioHelper.go (specifically in the FetchGothruFirstPano function), we replaced json.NewDecoder(...).Decode(...) with an approach that reads the bytes first, strips any BOM if present, and then unmarshals the clean payload.

// Read response bytes
bodyBytes, _ := io.ReadAll(apiRes.Body)

// Trim UTF-8 BOM
bodyBytes = bytes.TrimPrefix(bodyBytes, []byte{0xEF, 0xBB, 0xBF})

// Unmarshal safely
if err := json.Unmarshal(bodyBytes, &result); err != nil {
    return "", 0, err
}

This was applied to both the success path (HTTP 200) and the non-200 error-checking path (to safely extract the upstream message).

Part 2: Graceful Degradation for Thumbnail Failures

In src/action/v1/portfolio/createPhotographerPortfolio.go, the portfolio creation logic was refactored so that a failure in resolving the thumbnail does not abort the entire portfolio creation request.

Instead of propagating the error from resolveThumbnailForType, the system now catches it, logs a warning, and gracefully continues with an empty thumbnail pointer:

// Resolve thumbnail URL
thumbUrl, err := resolveThumbnailForType(pType, value)
if err != nil {
	fmt.Printf("Warning: failed to resolve thumbnail: %v\n", err)
	thumbUrl = ""
}

3. Acceptance Criteria Met

  • POST to /v1/photographers/{userId}/portfolio with type: 3 and a valid tourmkr URL succeeds (201 Created) even when the upstream first-pano API responds with a BOM-prefixed JSON.
  • Upstream JSON is now parsed BOM-tolerantly at both decode sites inside FetchGothruFirstPano.
  • A failure in thumbnail resolution no longer results in a 500 error; the system degrades gracefully and creates the portfolio item without a thumbnail.
  • All existing validation layers (type, title, duplicate tour keys) remain intact and fully functional.

Report

Creating a portfolio item fails with a 500 for (potentially) all users:

Request — POST {{base_url}}/v1/photographers/{{gothruUserId}}/portfolio

{ "type": 3, "title": "Bhopal", "value": "https://tourmkr.com/F1aK5Z1AR5" }

user_id: 19311

Response

{ "statusCode": 500, "requestTime": 31, "data": { "error": "invalid character 'ï' looking for beginning of value" } }

Root cause (confirmed in code)

invalid character 'ï' looking for beginning of value is the classic Go symptom of a UTF-8 BOM (EF BB BF → ï»¿) at the start of a JSON body that encoding/json is asked to decode.

For type: 3 (gothru) with a tourmkr.com value, portfolio creation resolves a thumbnail:

  • CreatePhotographerPortfolio → resolveThumbnailForType(pType, value) (src/action/v1/portfolio/createPhotographerPortfolio.go:78)
  • → FetchGothruFirstPano(tourkey, userId) calls the external API and decodes its response (src/action/v1/portfolio/portfolioHelper.go:337-365): 
    apiRes, _ := http.Get("https://apil.gothru.co/virtualtours/public/first-pano?tour_key=%s&user_id=%d")
...
if err := json.NewDecoder(apiRes.Body).Decode(&result); err != nil {
    return "", 0, err   // <- BOM in response body -> "invalid character 'ï' ..."
}

The upstream apil.gothru.co/virtualtours/public/first-pano response is BOM-prefixed (or otherwise non-clean JSON). json.Decode does not strip a BOM, so it errors; that error propagates up (resolveThumbnailForType → CreatePhotographerPortfolio → mapPortfolioError) and the entire create returns 500 — even though the thumbnail is optional. The same json.Unmarshal on the non-200 branch (portfolioHelper.go:349) has the same weakness.

Fix (two parts)

  1. Strip the BOM before decoding the upstream response:

    body, _ := io.ReadAll(apiRes.Body)
body = bytes.TrimPrefix(body, []byte{0xEF, 0xBB, 0xBF})
json.Unmarshal(body, &result)

    Apply to both decode sites in FetchGothruFirstPano (lines ~349 and ~363).

  2. Don't 500 the whole create on a thumbnail failure. Thumbnail is optional (thumbPtr may be nil). If resolveThumbnailForType fails, log and continue creating the portfolio with an empty thumbnail rather than aborting (createPhotographerPortfolio.go:78-81).

Acceptance criteria

  • POST …/portfolio with type:3 + a valid tourmkr URL succeeds (201) even when the first-pano API returns a BOM-prefixed body.
  • Upstream JSON is parsed BOM-tolerantly at both decode sites in FetchGothruFirstPano.
  • A thumbnail-resolution failure degrades to "no thumbnail" instead of a 500 (portfolio item still created).
  • Verified with the reported case (user 19311, https://tourmkr.com/F1aK5Z1AR5).
  • Existing validation (type/title/value, duplicate tour key) unchanged.
